phrases.intent_en: Ask when the market is open
phrases.text_local: À quelle heure ouvre le marché ?
phrases.pronunciation: ah kel UHR OO-vreh luh mar-SHAY
phrases.cultural_note: The covered market at Halle du Marché is open Tuesday to Sunday morning. It's closed on Mondays.

responses.phonetic: duh set uhr ah trez uhr, doo mar-DEE oh sahm-DEE
responses.local: De sept heures à treize heures, du mardi au samedi.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where to find mustard at the market
phrases.text_local: Où puis-je trouver de la moutarde ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo PWEE-zhuh troo-VAY duh lah moo-TARD
phrases.cultural_note: Dijon mustard is available at several stands in the market. Look for artisanal producers.

responses.english: There's an artisanal mustard stand in the center of the market.
responses.phonetic: eel ee ah uhn stahnd duh moo-TARD ar-tee-zah-NAL oh SAHN-truh doo mar-SHAY
responses.local: Il y a un stand de moutarde artisanale au centre du marché.

phrases.intent_en: Ask for a sample of local cheese
phrases.text_local: Puis-je avoir un échantillon de fromage local ?
phrases.pronunciation: PWEE-zhuh ah-VWAHR un ay-kahn-TEE-yon duh froh-MAHZH lo-KAL
phrases.cultural_note: Burgundy produces excellent cheeses: Epoisses, Chaource, and the famous Citeaux from the Cistercian abbey nearby.

responses.english: Yes, help yourself. We have Epoisses and Chaource.
responses.phonetic: wee, sair-VAY voo. ohn ah duh lay-PWAS ay doo shah-OORS
responses.local: Oui, servez-vous. On a de l'Epoisses et du Chaource.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where to find gingerbread from Dijon
phrases.text_local: Où puis-je trouver le pain d'épice de Dijon ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo PWEE-zhuh troo-VAY luh pan day-PEES duh dee-ZHON
phrases.cultural_note: Dijon gingerbread (pain d'épice) is a traditional specialty made with honey, not sugar. Look for the Annabelle brand at market stalls.

responses.english: It’s that way, just at the end of the street.
responses.phonetic: say par lah, zhoost oh boo duh lah roo
responses.local: C’est par là, juste au bout de la rue.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if the market has a café
phrases.text_local: Y a-t-il un café dans le marché ?
phrases.pronunciation: ee ah-TEEL un kah-FAY dan luh mar-SHAY
phrases.cultural_note: There's a small café inside the market hall — perfect for enjoying a coffee and watching the local vendors at work.

responses.english: Yes, at the back of the market, near the cheese shops.
responses.phonetic: wee, oh fohn doo mar-SHAY, preh day froh-mahzh-REE
responses.local: Oui, au fond du marché, près des fromageries.
